title: 2 Days in Berlin - Quick Summary
items: **Keep the two days geographically different.** Use Day 1 for Alexanderplatz, Museum Island, Unter den Linden, Brandenburg Gate and the Reichstag; use Day 2 for Bernauer Strasse, Mauerpark, the East Side Gallery and Kreuzberg.
**Start Day 1 at the World Clock on Alexanderplatz.** It gives you one exact meeting point and a clear view of the TV Tower, St. Mary's Church, Rotes Rathaus and the route into old Berlin.

**Choose one Museum Island interior, not three.** Most Staatliche Museen venues there close on Mondays, and the main Pergamonmuseum remains closed for construction, so check the official opening page before fixing the afternoon.
**Start Day 2 outside S Nordbahnhof.** Walk the Berlin Wall Memorial along Bernauer Strasse to Mauerpark, then use the M10 tram for the long handover to Warschauer Strasse.

**Treat Bernauer Strasse and the East Side Gallery as two different Wall sites.** Bernauer shows the border system and escape history; the East Side Gallery is a surviving Wall section painted after 1989.
**Book only the stops that can break the route.** Register for the Reichstag dome, reserve one museum when needed, and leave the rest flexible for weather, construction and tired legs.
